frame_analyses.description: Instrunments positioned to grasp the needle.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper grip at the stiffest part of the needle approximately 1cm from the tip.

frame_analyses.description: Suture being placed through the marks on the Penrose drain.
Suture wrapped around instrument to form the first throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper double wrap for the surgeon's knot.

frame_analyses.description: Knot being tightened for the first throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Ends pulled in opposite directions, successful cinching down of throw.

frame_analyses.description: Attempting the second throw with hand switch.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Hand switch was successful, single throw performed.
Small circular wrap with hand switch for securing the knot.

frame_analyses.description: Both ends of the suture being cut inside the trainer.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean cut performed without excessive length left.

knot_assessments.notes: Double wrap performed correctly and cinched down securely.
Single throw correctly performed with a hand switch.
Final throw secured successfully after hand switch.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: No visible gap after the knot is tightened, and the drain remains attached to the block.

technique_summary: The trainee demonstrated a competent understanding of intracorporeal suturing with smooth execution and proper hand switching between throws. Minor deviation observed at suture placement with minimal penalty incurred.
improvement_suggestions: Aim to reduce suture deviation at entry through marks.
Maintain the current efficiency while improving hand switch fluidity.
strengths: Effective knot security with proper surgeon's knot execution.
Completed task within a competitive time, demonstrating efficient skills.
